name: user.create_playable_test_level
title: Create Playable Test Level
description: Sample Python user tool that dry-runs or creates a new /Game/levels/<name> map with ground, basic lighting, PlayerStart, and an optional playable character.
wouldWrite: Create a new blank level under /Game/levels/<levelName> after refusing an existing asset when detection is available.
Spawn a static mesh ground surface.
Optionally spawn and auto-possess a character.
Save the new level and verify expected actor labels.

dryRun.description: If true, return wouldWrite preview without creating a level.

levelName.description: Asset name for the new map. The tool creates /Game/levels/<levelName> and rejects Lvl_TopDown.

includeCharacter.description: If true, spawn a character in the test level and set it to Auto Possess Player 0.

characterClassPath.description: Optional explicit Character-derived class path. If empty, the sample tries known ThirdPerson template classes, then /Script/Engine.Character.

characterLabel.description: Label for the optional spawned character.

groundSize.description: Ground square size in centimeters. Values below 500 are clamped to 500.

reason: Committed sample for the project-local Python user-tool track; not part of the canonical core registry.
notes: Sample only. Copy this directory to <ProjectDir>/Tools/UnrealMcpPyTools/create_playable_test_level/ before reload/smoke if you want it callable in an editor project.
